skill.name: prd
skill.description: Generate high-quality Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for software systems and AI-powered features. Includes executive summaries, user stories, technical specifications, and risk analysis.
skill.summary: Generate comprehensive PRDs with executive summaries, user stories, technical specs, and risk analysis.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation template for generating Product Requirements Documents. Static analysis flagged multiple false positives by misinterpreting markdown code blocks as executable code. No actual security risks detected - the skill contains only text templates and structured guidance.

content.value_statement: Transform vague feature ideas into structured, production-ready PRDs. This skill bridges business vision and technical execution with clear requirements, user stories, and success metrics.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured PRDs with executive summaries, user stories, and acceptance criteria
Define measurable success metrics and KPIs for features
Create AI system requirements and evaluation strategies
Produce technical specifications with architecture overviews
Identify risks and propose phased rollout plans
Facilitate discovery through targeted clarifying questions
limitations: Requires user input for discovery phase - cannot generate PRDs from nothing
Does not execute code or interact with external systems
Cannot validate technical feasibility of proposed solutions
Output quality depends on completeness of user-provided context

use_cases.title: Product Manager Planning New Feature
use_cases.description: A PM needs to translate a stakeholder request into a concrete technical specification for the engineering team.

use_cases.title: Engineer Documenting System Requirements
use_cases.description: A developer must document requirements for a new AI-powered feature including tool requirements and evaluation strategies.

use_cases.title: Startup Founder Validating Product Ideas
use_cases.description: A founder wants to structure their product vision into a formal document to share with potential co-founders and investors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PRD Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a PRD for a new search feature that allows users to find documentation using natural language questions.

prompt_templates.title: AI Feature with Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD for an intelligent code review assistant. Include AI system requirements, evaluation strategy with benchmark tests, and success metrics for code quality improvement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - AI-powered feature with testing requirements

prompt_templates.title: Full Product Launch
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive PRD for a SaaS analytics dashboard. Include user personas, technical architecture, integration points with third-party APIs, security considerations, and a phased rollout from MVP to v2.0.

prompt_templates.title: Discovery and Scoping Session
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an idea for a feature but I'm not sure about the requirements. Ask me clarifying questions about the problem, success metrics, and constraints, then help me draft a PRD based on my answers.

output_examples.input: Generate a PRD for a feature that summarizes long documents
output: Executive Summary: Users need to quickly understand key points from lengthy documents without reading everything. Solution: AI-powered summarization with configurable length and highlight extraction.
User Story: As a researcher, I want to get a 3-sentence summary of academic papers so I can decide if they're relevant to my work.
Success Metrics: Summaries must capture main thesis with 90% accuracy, generate in under 5 seconds, and support documents up to 50 pages.

output_examples.input: What should I include in the technical specifications section?
output: Architecture Overview: Describe data flow from user input through AI processing to output delivery.
Integration Points: List required APIs (authentication, database, external services) and their contracts.
Security & Privacy: Specify data handling requirements, encryption needs, and compliance considerations like GDPR or SOC2.

best_practices: Always conduct discovery before writing - ask at least 2 clarifying questions about the problem, metrics, and constraints.
Define requirements with concrete, measurable criteria instead of vague terms like 'fast' or 'intuitive'.
Include non-goals to protect scope and timeline from feature creep.
anti_patterns: Skipping the discovery phase and making assumptions about requirements.
Using vague language that cannot be tested or measured objectively.
Hallucinating constraints like tech stack or budget without user confirmation.

faq.question: What is a PRD and why do I need one?
faq.answer: A Product Requirements Document (PRD) is a structured specification that defines what you're building, for whom, and how you'll measure success. It serves as a single source of truth for all stakeholders.

faq.question: Can this skill generate a PRD without any input from me?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires discovery input about your specific problem, constraints, and success metrics. A good PRD cannot be generated from assumptions alone.

faq.question: What if I don't know the technical requirements?
faq.answer: The skill will help you identify what you need to know. Mark unknown items as 'TBD' and iterate with engineers to fill in technical details.

faq.question: Can this skill write code or interact with external systems?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates documentation only. It does not execute code, make API calls, or interact with external systems.

faq.question: How detailed should the acceptance criteria be?
faq.answer: Acceptance criteria should be specific enough that anyone can verify whether the feature is complete. Each user story should have 2-5 bullet points defining 'done'.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-software projects?
faq.answer: Yes. While designed for software and AI features, the PRD structure can be adapted for other product types by modifying the technical specifications section.
